articles.title: Saudis Lower July Oil Prices, Though Still at Decades High
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia cut the price of its main crude grade to Asia for a second straight month, though the premium for barrels to the kingdom’s largest market remained near the highest in decades.

articles.title: Korean Stocks Tumble as AI Trades Unwind, Threatening Bull Run
articles.summary: South Korean stocks plunged, imperiling the world’s biggest rally, as investors pulled back from artificial intelligence bets that have fueled the bull market in global equities.

articles.title: Flood Warnings for South China Farmlands Raise Rice-Crop Risks
articles.summary: Forecasts for heavy rains in southern China over the next week are raising the risk of flash floods, potentially inundating crops and damaging rice fields.

articles.title: Copper Rebounds From Friday Slump on China-Buying and US Flows
articles.summary: Copper edged up to recoup some of last week’s losses as buying activity in China and flows of metal to the US supported the demand outlook.

articles.title: Goldman’s Japan Chief Counts on New Leaders for Growth
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs Japan president Hidehiro Imatsu is promoting younger executives at the firm as a merger boom, rising stocks and volatile bond yields fuel the most intense talent war in years.

articles.title: Stocks Extend Selloff on Tech, Oil Climbs on Iran: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Stocks dropped as artificial-intelligence shares pulled back after a blistering rally and stronger-than-expected US jobs data reinforced bets for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes. Oil rose and bond

articles.title: Nvidia CEO Says Selloff in Tech Stocks Is a Buying Opportunity
articles.summary: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang called a global tech stocks selloff that began last week a buying opportunity, saying the buildout of artificial intelligence has just begun.

articles.title: Indonesia’s Foreign Reserves Suffer Longest Decline Since 2018
articles.summary: Indonesia’s foreign-exchange reserves fell for a fifth straight month in May, underscoring the cost of policymakers’ efforts to steady the rupiah after its slide to a record low.

articles.title: Goldman’s Moe Sees Korea Stock Rebound After ‘Scary’ Correction
articles.summary: South Korean stocks are expected to bounce back after a drop that triggered a circuit breaker, said Timothy Moe, Goldman Sachs chief Asia-Pacific regional equity strategist.

articles.title: China Coking Coal Hits Highest Since 2024 on Safety Shutdowns
articles.summary: Chinese coking coal rose to the highest since 2024 as the aftermath of a deadly mining accident and ongoing safety inspections kept prices elevated.
